page: Amazon Web Services m7g.metal is a general-purpose bare-metal server powered by 64 dedicated physical ARMv8 cores using the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 architecture. Operating at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz, this arm64 platform features 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it features a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities and high memory efficiency. The server achieved a top-tier rating in Geekbench multi-core testing and software compilation using Clang. Memory performance is a notable strength, with a top-tier Passmark memory score and a read-only small block memory bandwidth of 6209.33 GB/sec. Database operations also performed in the top tier, supported by strong Redis SET operations at 103.1 million operations per second and a static web serving throughput of 77.68 GB/sec. Conversely, single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core and LLM inference speeds, remain in the average tier.

From a resource density perspective, the bare-metal configuration offers high cost efficiency for multi-threaded workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading overhead. However, the lack of bundled local storage requires external storage solutions, which may introduce latency tradeoffs. The server is highly suitable for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, and high-throughput static web serving, while being less optimal for single-threaded tasks or workloads requiring local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services m7g.metal is a general-purpose bare-metal server featuring 64 dedicated physical ARMv8 cores based on the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 architecture. Operating at 2.6 GHz, it is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory, delivering 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps. The server lacks local storage and integrated GPUs. In benchmark evaluations, the m7g.metal demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, software compilation speeds, and memory bandwidth, alongside strong database and static web serving throughput. However, single-core performance and LLM inference speeds remain average. This bare-metal instance offers high resource density and cost efficiency for multi-threaded workloads, making it suitable for software development compilation, database operations, and high-throughput web serving, though workloads requiring local storage or GPU acceleration will face tradeoffs.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7g.metal is a bare-metal server with 64 dedicated ARMv8 Graviton cores, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and 30 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7g.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 64 ARMv8 Graviton cores, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and 30 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A bare-metal ARMv8 platform delivering dedicated 64-core compute and high-bandwidth DDR5 memory for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated physical ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 cores at 2.6 GHz
256.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory with 4.0 GB per core
Top-tier multi-core compilation and database benchmark performance
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput web serving
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware accelerators
Suited for multi-threaded applications, CI/CD, and database deployments
